stem: Find the average value of \( f(x,y) = [[p]]x + [[q]]y \) over the rectangle \( R = [0,[[b]]] \times [0,[[d]]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the integral \(\iint_R f\,dA\) itself, without dividing by the area of \(R\).
This evaluates \(f\) at the far corner \(([[b]],[[d]])\) instead of averaging.
This makes a sign error on the \(y\)-term of the average.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Let \(D\) be the triangular region \( 0 \le y \le [[m]]x,\ 0 \le x \le [[b]] \). Find the average value of \( f(x,y) = [[p]]x + [[q]]y \) over \(D\).
solution: The area of \(D\) is \( \int_{0}^{[[b]]}[[m]]x\,dx = [[areaT]] \). The average value is \( \frac{1}{[[areaT]]}\int_{0}^{[[b]]}\int_{0}^{[[m]]x}([[p]]x+[[q]]y)\,dy\,dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This evaluates \(f\) at the vertex \((x,y)=([[b]],[[m]][[b]])\) instead of averaging over \(D\).
This divides by \([[m]][[b]]\) (a rectangle's dimensions) instead of the true triangle area \([[areaT]]\).
This is twice the correct average (forgot the factor of \(\tfrac12\) in the triangle's area).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: A flat lamina occupies the rectangle \( R = [0,[[bb]]] \times [0,[[dd]]] \) and has density \( \delta(x,y) = [[r0]] + [[r1]]x + [[r2]]y \) (mass per unit area). Find the total mass of the lamina, \( \iint_R \delta(x,y)\,dA \).
distractor_rationales: This keeps only the constant part \([[r0]]\) of the density, ignoring the \(x\) and \(y\) terms.
This evaluates the density at the far corner and multiplies by the area, instead of integrating.
This drops the constant term \([[r0]]\cdot[[bb]]\cdot[[dd]]\) of the mass.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: A flat lamina occupies the triangular region \( D = \{(x,y): 0 \le y \le [[m]]x,\ 0 \le x \le [[b]]\} \) and has density \( \delta(x,y) = [[r0]] + [[r1]]x + [[r2]]y \). Find the total mass of the lamina.
solution: The mass is \( \int_{0}^{[[b]]}\int_{0}^{[[m]]x} ([[r0]]+[[r1]]x+[[r2]]y)\,dy\,dx = [[answer]] \), integrating the density over the triangular base.
distractor_rationales: This evaluates the density at the vertex and multiplies by the triangle's area, instead of integrating.
This keeps only the constant density term \([[r0]]\) times the triangle's area.
This forgets to divide by \(2\) anywhere in the triangle-area bookkeeping.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: A flat lamina occupies the rectangle \( R = [0,[[bb]]] \times [0,[[dd]]] \) and has density \( \delta(x,y) = [[r0]] + [[r1]]x + [[r2]]y \). Find the \(x\)-coordinate of its center of mass, \( \bar{x} = \dfrac{1}{M}\iint_R x\,\delta(x,y)\,dA \), where \(M\) is the total mass.
distractor_rationales: This uses the geometric midpoint of the rectangle, ignoring how the density is weighted.
This divides the rectangle's centroid-times-area by \(M\) instead of directly weighting by density.
This keeps only the constant part of the density in the numerator, dropping the \(x\) and \(y\) terms.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Find the average value of \( f(x,y) = [[c]](x^2+y^2) \) over the disk \( D: x^2+y^2 \le [[R2]] \).
solution: In polar coordinates \(x^2+y^2=r^2\), \(dA=r\,dr\,d\theta\), and \(\text{area}(D)=\pi[[R]]^2\). So the average value is \( \dfrac{1}{\pi[[R]]^2}\int_{0}^{2\pi}\int_{0}^{[[R]]} [[c]]\,r^2\cdot r\,dr\,d\theta = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses \(f\) evaluated at the boundary radius \(R\) directly rather than averaging over the whole disk.
This drops a factor of \(2\) somewhere in the radial antiderivative.
This forgets to divide by the area \(\pi R^2\), leaving an extra factor of \(R^2\).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
